name: Massa Sovada (Portuguese Sweet Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
3. Scald milk and add it to sugar, butter, and salt; stir until butter is melted.
4. Mix cinnamon with flour separately.
5. Add half of the flour mixture to the milk/sugar/butter/salt mixture and mix until smooth.
6. Beat eggs and add them along with the yeast to the mixture.
7. Continue adding remaining flour until a soft dough forms.
8. Remove dough from bowl and place on a floured surface.
9. Knead for about 15 minutes or until smooth and silky.
10. Shape into a ball and place in a buttered bowl.
11. Cover and let rise until doubled in size (2-1/2 to 3 hours).
12. Punch down risen dough and divide into two portions.
13. Place each portion in greased pans (8 inches round) and let rise again until doubled in size (1-1/2 to 2 hours).
14. Brush tops of dough with remaining beaten egg.
15. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 20-30 minutes or until golden brown.
16. Cool on wire racks.
